repo_notes.content: Dual-LLM / trust flow. The privileged orchestrator sees only T0-T2. The quarantined LLM is the ONLY consumer of raw T3 (web/email/file/tool-output) and emits ONLY schema-validated structured data — never tool calls, never free text fed back as instructions. All external content is tagged T3 at ingest. Never write 'the orchestrator reads a web page / processes tool output': T3 reaches the privileged side only as structured extraction via the T3->T2 dispatch chokepoint. Non-negotiable (PRD §5, DEC-007).
repo_notes.author: AlfredOS maintainers

repo_notes.content: Gateway sole-egress / connectivity-free core. The core runs on `internal:true` and cannot open external sockets (kernel-enforced). ALL egress — provider calls, tool egress, adapter egress — routes through the gateway L7 CONNECT proxy via the `EgressClient` seam. Never write that a provider adapter or plugin 'connects to' / 'makes an HTTP request to' an external host from the core.
repo_notes.author: AlfredOS maintainers

repo_notes.content: Self-modification reviewer-gate loop. Alfred improves itself via proposal branches in `/var/lib/alfred/state.git` (OUTSIDE the source tree) -> reviewer agent reviews diff+tests+rationale -> merge activates -> rollback is `git revert`. Never describe Alfred editing its own personas/skills/policy in place on main at runtime. Plugin install/remove needs additional human approval.
repo_notes.author: AlfredOS maintainers

repo_notes.content: Every action is hookable. Hookpoints wrap every side-effecting action (a PRD §5 non-negotiable invariant, ADR-0014). Describe capabilities as dispatched through hookpoints, not as direct un-interceptable calls.
repo_notes.author: AlfredOS maintainers

repo_notes.content: Secrets & outbound DLP. Plugins/tools request secret IDs from the broker; the broker substitutes the value at the tool-call boundary — never write 'the plugin reads the API key from an env var'. Every outbound path is DLP-scanned on by default and cannot be disabled per-call. (HARD rules 4 & 6.)
repo_notes.author: AlfredOS maintainers

repo_notes.content: Shipped-vs-scaffolded honesty. The security/infra chassis (Slices 1-4; gateway program Specs A/B/C) is complete and merged. The real-LLM engine is not fully wired: most paths are deterministic-echo; the exception is the agentic tool-calling epic (#339), in progress. Describe the architecture as built and tested; do NOT present tool-calling, live dual-LLM extraction, or end-to-end autonomous task execution as fully operational. When unsure whether something is shipped or planned, say 'is designed to', not 'does'.
repo_notes.author: AlfredOS maintainers

repo_notes.content: Functional-core / imperative-shell, no global state. Pure functions for transforms; classes only for state machines; dependencies injected via Protocols; fail-loud at trust boundaries. Don't describe modules as global singletons or ambient services.
repo_notes.author: AlfredOS maintainers

repo_notes.content: The model is not the perimeter; the tool layer is. Every security guarantee is enforced in code at the tool/transport boundary, independent of what any LLM is prompted or persuaded to do. Describe defenses as enforced by the boundary — never as 'the model is instructed to...'.
repo_notes.author: AlfredOS maintainers

repo_notes.content: Trust tiers are provenance, not capability; do not conflate the two tier axes. A plugin's subscriber-capability tier (system/operator/user-plugin) is orthogonal to a content trust tier (T0-T3). A plugin is never 'T2'; content is. Conflating them is a named bug class (tier-laundering) — keep the axes separate in all prose. (Highest-risk fidelity item; matches `src/alfred/security/capability_gate/policy.py`.)
repo_notes.author: AlfredOS maintainers

repo_notes.content: Vocabulary lock (with a gap fail-safe). `docs/glossary.md` is the single source of AlfredOS vocabulary; where a `##` heading exists (canonical, GitHub-slugified anchor), reuse the exact term and link to it, never coin a synonym. Some terms do NOT have a heading yet (reviewer gate, egress plane, capability grant, audit graph) — for those, describe the concept plainly in the page body and do NOT invent a definitional heading or fake an anchor link. Alfred = the default persona; AlfredOS = the system.
repo_notes.author: AlfredOS maintainers

repo_notes.content: Disclosure guardrail A — no 'how to weaken it' recipe. Describe controls and their threat model in full (Apache-2.0, public repo, Kerckhoffs — full design disclosure is correct). But do NOT compile a step-by-step checklist for disabling DLP, widening the egress allowlist, or lowering a grant; that is operator-runbook material, not public-wiki headline material.
repo_notes.author: AlfredOS maintainers

repo_notes.content: Disclosure guardrails B & C — residuals paired, live values never. (B) Whenever a page states a limitation or accepted residual, it must state, in the same breath, the assumption that puts it out of scope — e.g. 'this presupposes an attacker with code execution inside the core container, which is full compromise and outside the tool-layer's threat boundary.' A residual must read as documented accepted risk, never as an unpatched hole. (C) Describe canaries, allowlists, and secrets at the mechanism level; never enumerate literal canary strings, per-deployment seeding positions, real egress-allowlist domains, or anything token-shaped. C is model-enforced only — this steering file is authored in-repo (Devin's wiki generation reads whatever page/note text ships here), so guardrail C is backstopped by an input-side secret-shape scan over this file's own note/title/purpose text (see Validation), not by trusting the generator. Repo-wide secret hygiene remains gitleaks' job, not this scan's.
repo_notes.author: AlfredOS maintainers

pages.title: Overview
pages.purpose: Front-door router: a three-sentence pitch, the trust-boundary diagram, and an audience fork (self-hosters -> Security Model, operators -> Operating, contributors -> Extending).
page_notes: Ground in `PRD.md` §1-§2. Keep it a router, not a throat-clear.

pages.title: Architecture
pages.purpose: Structural spine — what it is, structurally.
page_notes: Ground in `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` and `PRD.md` §5 (the 8 non-negotiable invariants).

pages.title: Security Model
pages.purpose: The differentiator: how AlfredOS is secured.
page_notes: Ground in `docs/subsystems/security.md`, `docs/subsystems/quarantine.md`, and `PRD.md` §7.1.

pages.title: Operating & Deploying
pages.purpose: How to run it.
page_notes: Ground in `README.md` and `docs/runbooks/`.

pages.title: Extending & Contributing
pages.purpose: How to extend it.
page_notes: Ground in `CONTRIBUTING.md` and `docs/python-conventions.md`.

pages.title: Reference
pages.purpose: Lookups and honesty.
page_notes: Ground in `docs/glossary.md` and `docs/adr/`.

pages.title: Orchestrator & Act-Phase Loop
pages.purpose: The OODA turn and the agentic tool-calling loop that drives a persona's response.
page_notes: Ground in `PRD.md` §5, ADR-0003/0045/0046, and `src/alfred/orchestrator/`.
⚠ The real-LLM engine is not fully wired yet; #339 (agentic tool-calling) is in progress — this branch is PR3 of 4. Describe the act-phase loop as designed, not fully shipped.
No curated deep-doc exists yet — anchor to `PRD.md` §5 + `src/alfred/orchestrator/`; treat claims as unverified until a deep-doc lands.

pages.title: Memory Model (6 Layers)
pages.purpose: Working, episodic, summarized, semantic, vector, knowledge-graph layers and consolidation.
page_notes: No curated deep-doc exists yet — anchor to `PRD.md` §6.2 + `src/alfred/memory/`; treat claims as unverified until a deep-doc lands.
Working + episodic are live; semantic/vector/graph are partial/planned — describe as designed, not shipped.

pages.title: Personas & Addressing
pages.purpose: Persona registry, addressing modes, inter-persona bus with safety rails.
page_notes: No curated deep-doc exists yet — anchor to `PRD.md` §6.8 + `src/alfred/personas/`; treat claims as unverified until a deep-doc lands. No governing ADR either.
⚠ Only Alfred ships enabled; Lucius/Oracle/Diana are example persona definitions, not shipped personas.
Distinct from Providers & Routing: this page covers persona addressing (registry + addressing modes + inter-persona bus), not model routing.

pages.title: Providers & Routing
pages.purpose: Tiered provider routing, capability fallback, caching, tool-call protocol.
page_notes: Ground in `PRD.md` §6.5/§6.6, ADR-0001/0045/0046, and `src/alfred/providers/`.
No curated deep-doc exists yet — anchor to `PRD.md` §6.5 + `src/alfred/providers/`; treat claims as unverified until a deep-doc lands.

pages.title: Plugins & MCP Transport
pages.purpose: MCP plugin transport (stdio/HTTP), session lifecycle, manifest, capability declarations.
page_notes: Ground in `docs/subsystems/plugins.md`. The `comms_mcp` MCP transport is folded into this page, not a separate one.

pages.title: Comms & Gateway Inbound
pages.purpose: Comms adapters (Discord/TUI), identity binding, the gateway as comms front door + resume.
page_notes: Ground in `docs/subsystems/comms.md` and ADR-0036.
⚠ Discord and TUI are implemented; Telegram is an MVP-target adapter not yet built; Slack/WhatsApp/voice/email are post-MVP (`PRD.md` §6.1).

pages.title: Hooks & Hookpoints
pages.purpose: Every action is hookable; hook kinds, tiers, and the dispatcher.
page_notes: Ground in `docs/subsystems/hooks.md`, ADR-0014, and `PRD.md` §5/§5.1.
A PRD §5 non-negotiable invariant — kept as a first-class page, not folded into other prose.

pages.title: Connectivity-Free Core & Egress Topology
pages.purpose: Kernel-isolated core (internal:true); the gateway as sole external egress plane (L7 CONNECT proxy, default-deny allowlist).
page_notes: Ground in ADR-0040/0042/0043 and `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` (architecture overview, post de-stale).
Cross-linked from Security Model — the connectivity-free core / egress topology is both an architecture-identity page and a security control.

pages.title: Self-Modification: Reviewer-Gate Loop
pages.purpose: Proposal branches in /var/lib/alfred/state.git -> reviewer agent -> merge activates; rollback = revert.
page_notes: Ground in `PRD.md` §6.4 and ADR-0018/0021.

pages.title: Trust Tiers (T0-T3)
pages.purpose: Provenance tiers, boundary tagging, immutability; T3 minting capability-gated to a closed set of boundary call sites.
page_notes: Ground in `docs/subsystems/security.md`, ADR-0017 (trust-tier completion), and ADR-0028 (boot-time T3 nonce auth, folded in as principle, not wire-level mechanics).

pages.title: Dual-LLM Split & Quarantine
pages.purpose: Privileged orchestrator never sees raw T3; the quarantined LLM is the only T3 consumer; the single sanctioned T3->T2 dispatch chokepoint.
page_notes: Ground in `docs/subsystems/quarantine.md` and ADR-0046. The purpose MUST name the T3->T2 crossing.

pages.title: Capability Gate, Grants & Secret Broker
pages.purpose: Fail-closed gate, grants-before-dispatch, secret-IDs-not-values substituted at the tool boundary, out-of-band step-up for high-blast actions.
page_notes: Ground in `docs/subsystems/security.md`.
⚠ Reviewer-gated config step-up exists and is live; general high-blast tool step-up is more scaffold than a fully wired flow — don't present it as uniformly operational.

pages.title: DLP, Canary Tokens & Outbound Redaction
pages.purpose: On-by-default outbound DLP (per-call-undisableable), canary-token tripwires, redaction — the content-scanning family.
page_notes: Ground in `docs/subsystems/security.md`.
Mechanism-level description only — no literal canary strings, seeding positions, or real allowlist domains (disclosure guardrail C).

pages.title: Audit Log, Audit Graph & Reviewer Trail
pages.purpose: Append-only signed audit, alfred audit graph, and the reviewer-gate decision trail.
page_notes: No curated deep-doc exists yet — anchor to `PRD.md` §7.4 + `src/alfred/audit/`; treat claims as unverified until a deep-doc lands. No governing ADR either.

pages.title: Deployment & First-Run Setup
pages.purpose: Docker Compose stack, setup scripts, state.git seed, datastores (Postgres 18 / Redis 8 / Qdrant).
page_notes: Ground in `README.md`, `docker-compose.yaml`, and `bin/`.
⚠ README currently carries a 'Pre-implementation' status marker — carry that maturity caveat; don't present the quickstart as fully live.

pages.title: CLI & Operator Surface
pages.purpose: The alfred command tree by area (user, plugin, web, config, supervisor, audit, gateway).
page_notes: Anchor to the `.rulesync/rules/CLAUDE.md` command table (the committed source; root CLAUDE.md is a gitignored rulesync output Devin cannot see) + `src/alfred/cli/`.
Honour slice markers — alfred memory show / alfred cost report are Slice 4+ / not implemented.

pages.title: Configuration & Policies
pages.purpose: policies.yaml, reviewer-gated vs direct config keys, hot reload.
page_notes: Ground in `docs/subsystems/policies.md`.

pages.title: Writing Plugins, Personas & Skills
pages.purpose: Authoring MCP plugins, persona definitions, and runtime skills; manifest + capability model.

pages.title: Conventions & i18n

pages.title: Testing & the Adversarial Suite
pages.purpose: Unit/integration/smoke/adversarial layers; the adversarial suite is release-blocking.
page_notes: Ground in `PRD.md` §8.1.
Cross-linked from Security Model — the adversarial suite is security posture evidence, not just contributor detail.

pages.title: Glossary
pages.purpose: The single canonical AlfredOS vocabulary.
page_notes: Ground in `docs/glossary.md` — headings are canonical, GitHub-slugified anchors; reuse existing terms rather than coining synonyms.

pages.title: Decision Records (ADR Index)
pages.purpose: Index of the 47 ADRs; the load-bearing ones are pinned as page_notes on the concept pages they govern.
page_notes: Ground in `docs/adr/`. Load-bearing ADRs are pinned on the concept pages they govern: ADR-0040/0042/0043 -> Egress Topology, ADR-0017 -> Trust Tiers, ADR-0046 -> Dual-LLM Split, ADR-0045 -> Providers/Orchestrator, ADR-0014 -> Hooks.
ADR-0047 records the decision to adopt Devin DeepWiki — i.e. the decision behind this steering file itself.

pages.title: Slice / Roadmap Status
pages.purpose: Explicitly forward-looking page absorbing planned surfaces so no other page oversells them.
page_notes: Ground in `PRD.md` §9/§10 (MVP scope vs. roadmap, open questions) and the `.rulesync/rules/CLAUDE.md` current-state section (the committed source; root CLAUDE.md is a gitignored rulesync output).
